methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $18M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) in the same sector, with particular focus on 4 same-stage peers: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $18M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to next stage, yielding $36M. The peer-median method benchmarks against the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which show a median valuation of $175M with a range of $110M to $1.8B. The wide range reflects heterogeneity in market positioning and capital efficiency within the climate tech cohort.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $31M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $106M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($36M) and peer median ($175M) reflects tension between Dendra's capital deployed to date and valuations commanded by peer companies at equivalent stage. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, capital efficiency, and whether company positioning aligns more closely with higher-valued peers or the lower bound of the peer set.
